name: Rye Park Wind Farm
notable: Largest wind farm in NSW. 66x Vestas V162-6.0MW EnVentus — among highest capacity onshore turbines in Australia.

timeline.title: Tilt Renewables acquires project
timeline.detail: Tilt Renewables acquired development rights from Epuron

timeline.title: NSW planning approval
timeline.detail: Development consent granted for up to 92 turbines

timeline.title: EPBC Referral 2020/8837
timeline.detail: Rye Park Wind Farm — Controlled Action

timeline.title: Vestas and Zenviron contracts
timeline.detail: Vestas contracted for turbine supply/install; Zenviron awarded A$250M BoP contract

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

timeline.title: COD estimate updated
timeline.detail: COD changed from 2024-10 to 2024-10-07 (source: AEMO Generation Information)

eis_specs.document_title: Rye Park Wind Farm Environmental Impact Statement (NSW IPC SSD-8840)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: Maximum hub height in design envelope; V162 also offered at 95 m and 112 m
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual at hub height from WAsP modelling over 3-year met mast record — NSW Southern Tablelands
eis_specs.energy_yield_method: WAsP modelling with WRF mesoscale downscaling; P50 gross yield corrected for wake, availability and electrical losses
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Capital 330 kV substation (TransGrid), near Boorowa NSW
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~22 km of new 330 kV double-circuit transmission line from wind farm to TransGrid Capital substation.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: New 330 kV connection line required. Capital substation upgrade approved as part of SSD assessment. TransGrid connection works coordinated with Capital Wind Farm grid exit point.
eis_specs.notes: 396 MW (66 × 6.0 MW Vestas V162 EnVentus), NSW Southern Tablelands near Boorowa. Developer: Tilt Renewables (sold to APA Group 2022). NSW IPC SSD-8840 approved April 2021. Now operating (2023). BOP: Zenviron. V162-6.0 MW EnVentus is Vestas's largest onshore turbine; 66 turbines confirmed by Vestas supply contract. Noise constraint of 35 dBA(A) applies at nearest residences — stricter than NSW default due to low background noise in rural setting.
